<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Turn the Wheel of Seasons](/Feats.aspx?ID=4295) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Overflow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=506" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 35

</column>

---

The seasons shift rapidly. This impulse affects a cube 100 feet in each dimension within 1,000 feet. Choose the starting season. The impulse lasts for 4 rounds, proceeding to the next season at the start of each of your turns. You can't use this impulse again until the previous one ends.

 **Spring** Each ally in the cube gains 20 temporary HP that last until the start of your next turn. Any [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) ally rolls a [recovery check](/Rules.aspx?ID=375), but can't get worse than a success.

 **Summer** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100)) Each enemy in the area is exposed to sunlight and must attempt a Reflex save against your class DC. It's unaffected on a critical success, [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the start of your next turn on a success, or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until the start of your next turn on a failure.

 **Autumn** Leaves and rain make everything in the area [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) until the start of your next turn, and a cold wind makes each enemy in the area [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) until the start of your next turn unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save against your class DC.

 **Winter** Each enemy in the aura takes 5d6 cold damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC. A creature that fails its save also takes 2d6 [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
name: Turn the Wheel of Seasons

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ambush Bladderwort](/Feats.aspx?ID=4296) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 36

</column>

---

You plant the seed of a giant bladderwort in an unoccupied square of ground or water within 30 feet. It lasts for 10 minutes. Using this impulse again ends any previous one. If a creature enters its square, the seed erupts into a voluminous, translucent plant that seals tight around the triggering creature and fills with water. Unless the creature succeeds at a Reflex save against your class DC, it's [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) within the bladderwort and must hold its breath or begin [drowning](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). The [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC is also your class DC. The plant has AC 10 and 50 Hit Points.

 If a creature dies inside it, the plant shrinks down, converting itself and the remains into a watery fruit. A creature can eat this consumable to regain 1d8+4 HP, after which that creature is temporarily immune for 10 minutes. This fruit rots after 1 hour.

---
**Level (+4)** The bladderwort's HP increase by 25 and the fruit's healing increases by 1d8+4.
name: Ambush Bladderwort

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lava Leap](/Feats.aspx?ID=4297) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Overflow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=506" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 36

</column>

---

You wreath yourself in molten stone and hurtle toward your enemy. [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) up to your Speed. At the end of your Leap, a wave of lava crashes onto all creatures in a 10-foot emanation. Each creature in the area takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6 fire damage, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC.

 The cooling remains of the lava form a temporary protective shell around you, granting you a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

---
**Level (+3)** Each type of damage increases by 1d6.
name: Lava Leap

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Living Bonfire](/Feats.aspx?ID=4298) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 36

</column>

---

Roots and branches of living wood writhe in elemental fire at your command, forming a bonfire fearsome enough to scare off predators in the night. You conjure a bonfire in an unoccupied 10-foot-square space within 30 feet. The bonfire burns for 10 hours, providing all the benefits of a normal campfire. If you use this impulse again, any previous one ends.

 When you make a wood ranged [_Elemental Blast_](/Actions.aspx?ID=2125), you can have it come from the bonfire instead of you, flinging burning logs. This blast deals an additional 1d6 fire damage. Each time you do this, the size of the bonfire is reduced by one 5-foot square. If all the squares are removed, the impulse ends.

---
**Level (+5)** The fire damage increases by 1d6.
name: Living Bonfire

    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rain of Rust](/Feats.aspx?ID=4299) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 36

</column>

---

You conjure a red raincloud in a 10-foot burst within 60 feet, which pours rust-colored rain directly below it. Any creature in the rain with the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait, that is made of metal, or is wearing metal armor is covered in corrosive build-up. Any such creature is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) as long as it remains in the area. If such a creature is in the rain at the start of its turn, it takes 3d6 damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save against your class DC, plus 1d6 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) if it fails. Damage from this impulse ignores Hardness. The cloud lasts 1 minute but ends if you use the impulse again.

---
**Level (+2)** The initial damage increases by 1d6.
name: Rain of Rust

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Whirling Grindstone](/Feats.aspx?ID=4300) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=15" />
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 36

</column>

---

A whirling grindstone made of flint appears in an unoccupied square within 30 feet. The grindstone shreds flesh and shoots sparks. Attempt an impulse attack roll against the AC of a creature adjacent to the grindstone. On a hit, the creature takes 2d6 slashing damage and 1d6 fire damage (or double damage on a critical hit). The grindstone lasts until the end of your next turn, and you can Sustain the impulse up to 1 minute. On subsequent turns, the first time you Sustain the impulse that turn, you can roll the grindstone up to 20 feet and can repeat the attack.

 A creature within reach of the grindstone can Interact with it to sharpen a metal weapon. This grants a +2 circumstance bonus to the next damage roll made with that weapon within 1 minute.

---
**Level (+5)** The grindstone's damage increases by 1d6 slashing and 1d6 fire, and the bonus to weapons increases by 1.
name: Whirling Grindstone

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ash Strider](/Feats.aspx?ID=4301) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Overflow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=506" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 36

</column>

---

You discorporate into a cloud of whirling ash and Stride. If you have a fly Speed, you can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) instead. This movement doesn't trigger reactions, you can move through occupied spaces and tiny cracks, and you ignore any [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain that wouldn't impede smoke. The first creature you pass through during this movement takes 3d6 fire damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC. Ash lingers around you after your movement is complete, granting you [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) until the start of your next turn.

---
**Level (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Ash Strider

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />

</traits>

---

A vortex of sand and dust surrounds you. Each creature inside your kinetic aura (including you) is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from any creature outside your kinetic aura, and creatures outside the aura are concealed from creatures inside the aura other than you. Your air impulses carry sands that cut at great speed. When you use a damaging air [impulse](/Traits.aspx?ID=502) that affects at least one creature in your kinetic aura, that impulse deals 1 additional slashing damage, or 2 if the impulse has a single target.

---
**Level (+2)** The extra damage increases by 1, or 2 if the impulse has a single target.
name: Desert Wind

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Elemental Artillery](/Feats.aspx?ID=4303) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=540" />
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 36

</column>

---

Spinning wood and metal together, you create a rugged wooden ballista. The ballista is Medium and appears in an unoccupied space within 30 feet. It immediately shoots a bolt with a jagged tip of elemental metal. Make an impulse attack roll against the AC of a target within 120 feet. The target takes 3d12 piercing damage on a hit (or double damage on a critical hit).

 The ballista can be shot again, but it must first be reloaded with two Interact actions. The ballista lasts until the end of your next turn, and you can Sustain the impulse. Each time you Sustain it, you can roll the ballista up to 20 feet, shoot it if it's loaded, or contribute 1 action toward reloading it.

---
**Level (+3)** The damage increases by 1d12.
name: Elemental Artillery

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

You conjure up to six cube-shaped mounds of packed earth. Each appears in an unoccupied square within 120 feet, fills its square, and provides cover. A mound has AC 10, Hardness 10, and 20 HP, and is immune to critical hits and precision damage. If destroyed, a mound becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). The mounds last for an unlimited duration, but if you use the impulse again, any previous one ends.

 Sharpened wooden stakes protrude from each mound into adjacent squares. They can project from any of its sides; you choose which sides for each mound. For each square of wooden stakes a creature enters, that creature takes 2d6 piercing damage. Destroying a mound also destroys its stakes.

---
**Level (+2)** The HP of each section of the wall increases by 10, and the piercing damage increases by 1d6.
name: Jagged Berms

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rising Hurricane](/Feats.aspx?ID=4307) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Overflow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=506" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 37

</column>

---

A hurricane lifts your enemies into the air before bringing them crashing down in a bloody rain. Your hurricane appears in a cylinder that's 40 feet tall, has a 30-foot diameter, and is within 120 feet. All creatures in the area take 2d6 bludgeoning damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save against your class DC. Lift any creature that fails its save to any height you choose within the area, move it up to 5 feet in any direction, then drop it. It takes falling damage normally unless it has a fly Speed.

---
**Level (+3)** The rain damage increases by 1d6, and the cylinder's height increases by 5 feet.
name: Rising Hurricane

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Roiling Mudslide](/Feats.aspx?ID=4308) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 37

</column>

---

You form water and earth into a mudslide that smashes your opponents and coats them in mud. Each creature in a 30-foot cone takes 2d8 bludgeoning damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save against your class DC. A creature that fails is also [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet (or 10 feet on a critical failure) and coated in mud until the end of its next turn. While coated in mud, the creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) at the end of its movement any time it ends a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action other than a Crawl or Step. The creature can attempt an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check or Reflex save against your class DC, avoiding the fall if it succeeds.

---
**Level (+4)** The damage increases by 1d8.
name: Roiling Mudslide

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 37 --- You shape your kinetic aura into swirling armor of steam that scalds your enemies and propels you on super-heated jet streams. A creature can take damage from Steam Knight only once per round. Your steam armor has the following effects.
  • You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed.
  • When you [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), you can jump up to your Speed. You don't immediately fall at the end of a jump, provided you Leap again with your next action. If you Leap over a creature and come within 10 feet, that creature takes 2d6 fire damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC.
  • At the start of each of your turns, you can emit steam as a free action. It deals 2d6 fire damage to each creature in your kinetic aura with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC. A creature that fails is also [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet.
--- **Level (+5)** The fire damage from a jump or blast of steam increases by 1d6.
name: Steam Knight

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 37 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) A Tree of Duality creates both the Cleansing Pollen and Hallucinogenic Spore effects, not just one._ --- An ephemeral tree bursts forth, floating on air in an unoccupied square of your choice within 60 feet. Blooming flowers and fungal growths shed pollen and spores, which a swirling gust of air spreads in a 10-foot emanation around it. The tree lasts until the end of your next turn, and you can Sustain it up to 1 minute.
  • **Cleansing Pollen** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) Each living ally that's in the area or enters it regains 3d4 HP and is then temporarily immune to regaining HP from Tree of Duality for 10 minutes.
  • **Hallucinogenic Spores** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Enemies in the area are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7). An enemy that leaves the area remains dazzled until the start of its next turn.
--- **Level (+2)** The healing increases by 1d4.
name: Tree of Duality

    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<trait label="Lineage" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=317" />
</traits>

</column>

---

Your elemental heritage is reflected in the oils and fragrances of plants, in tree resin that fossilizes into amber, or in the gentle smell of a flower. Sticky, golden sap runs through your veins instead of blood. Each time a creature deals slashing or piercing damage to you with a melee Strike, your sap coats its weapon or [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack. The creature takes a –1 circumstance penalty on attack rolls with that weapon or unarmed attack until the end of its turn.
name: Ambersoul

 **Decompose** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Void energy seeps out of you, decaying everything within a 5-foot emanation and causing plants and foliage to age and decompose. Natural [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) is destroyed, and creatures in the area with the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait take 1d6 void damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher.
name: Moldersoul

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

You gain nourishment through photosynthesis, like trees and other green plants. You typically don't need to pay for food, though you begin to [starve](/Rules.aspx?ID=537) if you go without sunlight for 1 week. You can derive nourishment from specially formulated bottles of sunlight instead of natural sunlight, but these bottles cost 10 times as much as standard rations (4 gp per week).
name: Sunlit Vitality

Denizens of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) take immense pride in the magnificent works of art they can craft from wood, and practicing your own woodcraft helps you feel a connection with your planar heritage. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4). If you would automatically become trained in Crafting (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You gain the [Specialty Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=844) skill feat for woodworking.
name: Woodworker

    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Ardande" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=498" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />

<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 48

**Requirements**
You are standing on the ground, and there are plants rooted in the ground within 100 feet of you.

</column>

---

The root systems of trees are a far-reaching network of information, and you know how to access them and extract their secrets. You [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) within 30 feet using [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40), an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) sense, instead of one of your own senses. You can also detect subjects touching or burrowing through the surface you are standing on in this way.
name: Read the Roots
requirement_markdown: You are standing on the ground, and there are plants rooted in the ground within 100 feet of you.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wood Ward](/Feats.aspx?ID=4323) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Ardande" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=498" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 49

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Trigger**
A creature targets you with an attack.

</column>

---

With a sweep of your hand, vines and roots burst from the ground along the edge of your space between you and the attacker, creating a natural lattice of wood that grants [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459). The circumstance bonus from the cover applies to your AC when you're determining the outcome of the triggering attack. If the triggering attack still hits and deals physical damage to you, the ward reduces the damage by 30 and is immediately destroyed. If the attack misses, the ward instead persists for 3 rounds before withering back into the earth. Creatures can cross the ward, but it's [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).
name: Wood Ward

[Ferrousoul](/Feats.aspx?ID=4324) <actions string="" />
</title>

<trait label="Talos" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=507" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 52

</column>

---

Whether in their pure elemental forms or as part of an alloy such as steel, the elemental metal in your soul resonates strongly with iron, bronze, and other sturdy, practical metals suitable for weaponry. Your fist attacks gain the [modular B, P, or S](/Traits.aspx?ID=271) trait and lose the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait. Your fist's damage die increases to 1d6.
name: Ferrousoul

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gildedsoul](/Feats.aspx?ID=4325) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Lineage" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=317" />
<trait label="Talos" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=507" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 52

</column>

---

Your elemental lineage manifests in the polished gleam of precious metals, such as silver and gold, or even rare and valuable skymetals like [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=276). This natural luster enhances your charm; you become trained in your choice of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14). If you would automatically become trained in both these skills (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. If you're trained in Society, you also gain the [Courtly Graces](/Feats.aspx?ID=772) skill feat.
name: Gildedsoul

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quicksoul](/Feats.aspx?ID=4326) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Lineage" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=317" />
<trait label="Talos" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=507" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 52

</column>

---

The elemental metal in your bloodline literally courses through your veins in the form of liquid metals like mercury, rubidium, gallium, and [djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410), giving your actions a languid fluidity. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1). If you would automatically become trained in Acrobatics (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. As these metals are largely toxic to organic life, you also gain the Toxic Touch action.

 **Toxic Touch** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** Your most recent action was to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29), and you successfully moved through an enemy's space; **Effect** Make a melee [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strike against the enemy whose space you moved through. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (or takes 2d6 persistent poison and is sickened 2 on a critical hit).
name: Quicksoul

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Conductor's Redirection](/Feats.aspx?ID=4328) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Talos" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=507" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 53

**Trigger**
You are hit by an attack, spell, or other effect that deals electricity damage.

</column>

---

You conduct the damage through your body, taking damage as normal (if applicable) and redirecting it at one target within 10 feet that you can see. That target takes an equal amount of damage with a basic Fortitude save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. The damage they take is based on the triggering damage, not modified by your degree of success, resistances, and so forth.
name: Conductor's Redirection

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kineticist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4333) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
<trait label="Multiclass" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=115" />
</traits>

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 57

**Archetype** [Kineticist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=206)

**Prerequisites**
Constitution +2

</column>

---

You become trained in kineticist class DC and impulse attack rolls. Choose one element to be your kinetic element (air, earth, fire, metal, water, or wood). You gain a kinetic aura and the [Channel Elements](/Actions.aspx?ID=2124) action, though you don't get to use an Elemental Blast or stance impulse when you take that action. You gain the [Elemental Blast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2125) action. Your Elemental Blast does not automatically gain additional damage dice every four levels, instead requiring you to take the [Improved Elemental Blast](/Feats.aspx?ID=4337) feat. You become trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43); if you were already trained in Nature, you instead become trained in another skill of your choice.

<title level="2">Kineticist Dedication leads to...</title>
[Base Kinesis](/Feats.aspx?ID=4334), [Expert Kinetic Control](/Feats.aspx?ID=4339), [Improved Elemental Blast](/Feats.aspx?ID=4337), [Through the Gate](/Feats.aspx?ID=4335)
name: Kineticist Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Elementalist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4340) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 59

**Archetype** [Elementalist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=207)

**Prerequisites**
elemental magic

</column>

---

Each day when you make your daily preparations, you can attune yourself to one element of your choice from your elemental philosophy. You gain resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1 resistance) against damage dealt by effects with your attuned elemental trait. This attunement lasts until you next make your daily preparations.

<title level="2">Elementalist Dedication leads to...</title>
[Burning Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4343), [Current Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4344), [Dousing Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4341), [Elemental Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=4342), [Expanded Elemental Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=4345), [Growth Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4348), [Metabolize Element](/Feats.aspx?ID=4346), [Redirect Elements](/Feats.aspx?ID=4350), [Reverberating Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4347), [Rockslide Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4349), [Water Step](/Feats.aspx?ID=4351), [Wind-Tossed Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4352)
name: Elementalist Dedication

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dousing Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4341) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Spellshape" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=513" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 59

**Archetype** [Elementalist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=207)

**Prerequisites**
[Elementalist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4340); water is in your elemental philosophy

</column>

---

You enhance your spell with elemental water, soaking the target. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell targeting a single creature, you soak the target of the spell with water. If the target has [persistent acid or fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), the DC to end those conditions is reduced to 10, and the creature can attempt a flat check to end those types of persistent damage immediately. The spell gains the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait (causing it to deal extra damage to creatures with weakness to water).
name: Dousing Spell
prerequisite_markdown: [Elementalist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4340); water is in your elemental philosophy

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 59 **Archetype** [Elementalist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=207) **Prerequisites** [Elementalist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4340); [Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=604) --- Your familiar becomes an elemental spirit capable of taking on aspects of the elements. Other than taking the form of an elemental instead of an animal, this familiar continues to use all the same rules as other familiars. If you want to give your familiar the elemental familiar abilities found on page 42, you must select that familiar ability. Your familiar gains one additional familiar ability each day, which must be one of the following elemental familiar abilities. While your familiar has an elemental familiar ability, your familiar is composed of the associated elemental matter and gains the matching elemental trait. You can't select more than one elemental familiar ability at a time.
  • **Air** If your familiar stays completely still for 1 round, it becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) until it next takes an action. Any motion, even being moved or carried by another creature, ends this effect.
  • **Earth** Your familiar gains resistance to physical damage (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) equal to half your level.
  • **Fire** Your familiar sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet) and emits warmth. Creatures who remain within a 15-foot emanation don't take damage from [severe environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642).
  • **Metal** Your familiar gains resistance to physical damage from metal weapons equal to half your level.
  • **Water** Your familiar can move through a gap at least 2 inches wide without [Squeezing](/Actions.aspx?ID=31) and can Squeeze through a gap at least 1 inch wide.
  • **Wood** If your familiar remains in bright light for 1 round, it gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) equal to half your level. Once it is out of bright light, this effect ends.
name: Elemental Familiar

This has no effect if the spell already deals persistent fire damage. The spell gains the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) trait.

[Current Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=4344) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Spellshape" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=513" />

**Prerequisites**
[Elementalist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4340); air or water is in your elemental philosophy

</column>

---

As you use your magic to manipulate air or water, you divert some of its currents to form a barrier around you. If your next action is to Cast a Spell with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait, until the start of your next turn, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC or a +2 circumstance bonus against ranged attacks. This effect has the air or water trait, or both, depending on the traits of the spell you cast. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to all saves against effects with the air trait, water trait, or both until the start of your next turn, depending on the spell's traits.
name: Current Spell
prerequisite_markdown: [Elementalist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4340); air or water is in your elemental philosophy

<trait label="Barbarian" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=18" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Rage" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=135" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 54

**Prerequisites**
[elemental instinct](/Instincts.aspx?ID=7)

**Requirements**
You're [raging](/Actions.aspx?ID=3), and you haven't used this ability since you last Raged.

</column>

---

You unleash the energy roiling within you, exploding elemental matter in a 15-foot emanation. Each creature in the area takes 1d8 damage per level you possess, with the same type you chose for elemental rage. Each creature in the area must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC. Elemental Explosion gains the trait of your element.
name: Elemental Explosion
requirement_markdown: You're [raging](/Actions.aspx?ID=3), and you haven't used this ability since you last Raged.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Worm Caller Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4357) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 80

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Shake it Off is a reaction_

**Archetype** [Worm Caller](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=208)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) or Trained in [Cave Worm Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

You pledge yourself to understanding and emulating one of the most powerful creatures of the Darklands: the mighty [cave worm](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=21). You gain the following action.

 **Shake It Off** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail or critically fail a saving throw against a condition or adverse effect (such as [_baleful polymorph_](/Spells.aspx?ID=17)); **Effect** You reroll the triggering save and use the better result.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Worm Caller archetype.

<title level="2">Worm Caller Dedication leads to...</title>
[Azure Fins](/Feats.aspx?ID=4363), [Call Worm Spirit](/Feats.aspx?ID=4364), [Crimson Breath](/Feats.aspx?ID=4366), [Inexorable](/Feats.aspx?ID=4360), [Tunnel](/Feats.aspx?ID=4365), [Venomous Touch](/Feats.aspx?ID=4367), [Worm Empathy](/Feats.aspx?ID=4362), [Worm Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=4368), [Worm Sense](/Feats.aspx?ID=4358), [Worm's Feast](/Feats.aspx?ID=4361), [Wormskin](/Feats.aspx?ID=4359)
name: Worm Caller Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) or Trained in [Cave Worm Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Wormskin](/Feats.aspx?ID=4359) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Worm Caller](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=208)

**Prerequisites**
[Worm Caller Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4357)

</column>

---

Your flesh grows thick and bloated, protecting you against harm. You gain resistance equal to half your level versus one of the following types of damage, chosen when you take the feat: fire, cold, or bludgeoning. This choice cannot be changed.

 When you're unarmored, your flesh gives you a +2 item bonus to AC with a Dexterity cap of +3. You can apply with armor potency runes and property runes directly to your flesh, though excruciatingly painful.

**Special** You can take this feat up to three times, each time selecting a new damage type from the list.
name: Wormskin

You imbue yourself with the resilience of the [cave worm](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=21). When you gain the [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) condition, roll a DC 15 flat check. On a success you ignore the condition.

 When using [Worm Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=4368) you are immune to penalties to your Speed and the immobilized condition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you, and you ignore [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain.

You can connect with [cave worms](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=21) (and other worm-related creatures, at the GM's discretion) on a rudimentary level. You can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on cave worms and to make simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of them. In most cases, the cave worm's starting attitude is indifferent and they will give you time to establish contact.

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 81 **Archetype** [Worm Caller](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=208) **Prerequisites** [Worm Caller Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4357) --- You take on the form of a purple worm or transform into a humanoid-worm hybrid, retaining your own head but with a worm's body, transforming into a Huge battle form. You must have enough space to expand into or the ability is lost. While in this form, you gain the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) trait. You can Dismiss the form. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of which battle form you choose:
  • AC = 20 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 40 temporary Hit Points.
  • [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • One or more [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) melee attacks specific to the battle form you choose, which are the only attacks you can Strike with. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +25, and you use the listed damage. Melee attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition, for example). If your unarmed attack modifier is higher, you can use it instead.
  • [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +30, unless your own modifier is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the type of monster you choose:
  • **Purple Worm** Speed 40 feet, burrow 30 feet, swim 20 feet; **Melee** jaws ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+15 piercing; **Melee** stinger ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+15 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent poison](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); **Melee** body ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)) **Damage** 1d10+13 bludgeoning
  • **Hybrid** Speed 40 feet; **Melee** stinger ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+15 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent poison](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); **Melee** body ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d10+13 bludgeoning; **Hybrid Caster** You retain the ability to speak, and can cast spells as long as they don't have somatic or material components.
name: Worm Form

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stone Brawler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=4371) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 82

**Archetype** [Stone Brawler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=209)

**Prerequisites**
Constitution 14

</column>

---

You've learned to call the primal power of elemental stone to your aid. You gain the Stonestrike Stance action.

 **Stonestrike Stance** <actions string="Single Action" /> **Requirements** You are standing on the ground; **Effect** You enter the stance of unyielding stone and draw upon the power of the living rock, encasing your fists in stone and allowing you to make stonestrike attacks. These deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage, are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and have the [forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the stone brawler archetype.

<title level="2">Stone Brawler Dedication leads to...</title>
[Ore Fists](/Feats.aspx?ID=4374), [Stonebane](/Feats.aspx?ID=4375), [Stoney Deflection](/Feats.aspx?ID=4373), [Stoney Skin](/Feats.aspx?ID=4372)
name: Stone Brawler Dedication
